data.title: CEO Bill Hutchinson of Dunhill Partners acquires Northpark Village Shopping Center in Lubbock, a 70,479-square-foot asset.
data.description: On January 9, 2026, Dunhill Partners, Inc., a prominent player in high-performing real estate investments, revealed the acquisition of the Northpark Village Shopping Center. This significant move involves a 70,479-square-foot property that goes beyond a mere real estate deal, signaling a strategic investment by the company.

data.title: LMU CBA marks centennial year at SoFi Stadium on Jan. 4, hosted by Office Beacon, celebrating 100 years of excellence.
data.description: On January 4, 2026, Loyola Marymount University's College of Business Administration (CBA) kicked off its 100th anniversary at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles. The event, co-hosted with Office Beacon, marked the beginning of the centennial celebrations for LMU. Important figures participated in the gathering to commemorate this milestone ...

data.title: Bitcoin is showing signs of a rally in 2026, with profit-taking pressure easing and prices rebounding from $87,000 to the mid-$90,000 range. There is significant overhead supply between $92,000 and $117,000.
data.description: Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) is showing promising signs for a potential rally in 2026, supported by positive technical indicators and diminishing selling pressure. According to Glassnode data, the pressure to take profits has decreased significantly, enabling Bitcoin to bounce back from $87,000 to the mid-$90,000 level. This rebound indicates a decrease in selling activity, especially among long-term holders, and signifies a period of stabilization after a distribution phase observed in the fourth quarter of 2025. However, the upward momentum may encounter resistance due to significant selling pressure in the range of $92,000 to $117,000.

data.title: Controversy at Polymarket as they disregard rules on Venezuela invasion, causing backlash from traders who lost millions.
data.description: Polymarket is under scrutiny following controversial rulings on multiple markets that have resulted in significant losses for traders. One such ruling involved the Venezuela market, where Polymarket determined that the U.S. did not invade Venezuela despite a military operation that included 150 aircraft from 20 bases, resulting in 100 casualties and the removal of the country's leader. This decision contradicted the platform's own rules, which stated that a military offensive by the U.S. to establish control over any part of Venezuela would result in a "Yes" resolution. Despite President Trump's statements claiming U.S. control over Venezuela, the market initially saw a sharp increase from 2.5 cents to over 50 cents, sparking backlash from traders. This story can be found on Benzinga.com for more details.

data.title: Sangbo Sim (57) appointed as CFO of ZKW Group GmbH, replacing Sangwook Woo. Sim brings international financial experience.
data.description: In Wieselburg, Austria, on January 9, 2026, Sangbo Sim, aged 57, assumed the role of CFO at ZKW Group GmbH starting from January 1, 2026. He has a wealth of international financial experience and is replacing Sangwook Woo. Woo, who is relocating to Korea as scheduled. Prior to joining ZKW Group GmbH, Sim had a diverse career at LG.
US Commerce Department will release report on Personal Income for December on February 20th.

data.title: Latin America Industrial Cleaning Products Market to reach USD 22.1 billion by 2034 with 6.5% CAGR growth, up from USD 11.8 billion in 2024 - ResearchAndMarkets.
data.description: A recent report on the industrial cleaning products market in Latin America states that it was worth USD 11.8 billion in 2024. The market is projected to expand at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 6.5%, aiming to reach USD 22.1 billion by 2034. The growth in the sector is attributed to heightened cleanliness standards and stringent sanitation policies implemented throughout the region.

data.title: Vera Therapeutics awards inducement grants following Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4) with undisclosed number.
data.description: A recent study found that around 70% of people believe that the government is hiding information about extraterrestrial life. The study surveyed 1000 individuals across various demographics. The data revealed that 80% of those surveyed felt that the government should be more transparent about the subject. Additionally, 60% of respondents claimed to have personally witnessed unidentified flying objects. The study raises questions about public trust in government disclosure regarding extraterrestrial life.

data.title: Preston Dermatology in NC wins Gold for Best Dermatologist & Bronze in DIAMOND Awards. Dr. Solomon honored.
data.description: Dr. Sheel Desai Solomon, who is a board-certified dermatologist, and her practice called Preston Dermatology & Skin Surgery Center in North Carolina, were recently recognized with the Gold award for Best Dermatologist by 5 West Magazine. This prestigious honor was conferred upon them for their exceptional services in the western Triangle region.

data.title: Andreessen Horowitz secures $15B to drive tech advancements, bolster US in tech race.
data.description: One of the leading cryptocurrency investors, Andreessen Horowitz, has successfully secured a substantial amount of $15 billion through various funds. The primary aim behind this significant fundraising is to drive investments in technology initiatives and play a pivotal role in enhancing the prospects of America's technological landscape.

data.title: Boyd Group Services Inc. acquires Joe Hudson's Collision Center, solidifying its position in the industry.
data.description: Boyd Group Services Inc. (TSX: BYD) (NYSE: BGSI) has strengthened its position as a prominent player in the North American collision industry with a recent acquisition. The company, known as BGSI or simply Boyd, finalized the acquisition of Joe, marking a significant milestone. This move showcases Boyd's commitment to growth and solidifies its reputation in the industry. This development comes amidst a rapidly evolving market landscape, underlining Boyd's strategic approach to expansion. The acquisition aligns with Boyd's ambitious goals and underscores its focus on enhancing market presence and capabilities.

data.title: Tilray's Q3 earnings beat expectations with $217.51 million revenue & $67.5 million in cannabis sales. Analysts say valuation looks attractive.
data.description: Tilray Brands Inc. (NASDAQ: TLRY) exceeded expectations in its quarterly report, posting earnings of one cent per share compared to the anticipated loss of 20 cents. Quarterly revenue totaled $217.51 million, surpassing the estimated $210.95 million. The breakdown showed a 3% increase in Cannabis net revenue reaching $67.5 million, Beverage net revenue at $50.1 million, and Wellness net revenue totaling $14.6 million. The growth in Cannabis sales was attributed to a 36% rise in international cannabis and a 6% increase in Canadian adult-use cannabis. Distribution net revenue, which includes Tilray Pharma, reached a record quarter revenue of $85.3 million. Tilray Medical U.S. also aims to capitalize on existing infrastructure for future growth.

data.title: Over 3,000 workers at Chicago medical centers demand fair contracts, to hold press conference on Jan 10.
data.description: More than 3,000 employees at Rush University Medical Center and University of Chicago Medical Center, who are being represented by Teamsters Local 743, are urging for fair contract agreements. A press conference has been scheduled for Saturday, Jan. 10th at 3 p.m. to address the current situation.
